texto: Death does not come when the body is too exausted to live.Death comes because the brilliance inside of us can only be contained for so long.We do not die.We pass on.Pass on the life burning through our throats.When you leave me, I will not cry for you.I will run into the strongest wind I can find and welcome you home.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Lee sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Death does not come when the body is too exausted to live.Death comes because the brilliance insi…” articula vida, body, death em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como luto e death.
